title: Crispy Saudi Arabian Flour Flatbread
description: Malty, snappy flatbread with red wine vinegar and a gentle rise, perfect for kids and easy on the blood sugar.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine whole wheat flour, all-purpose flour, instant yeast, salt, and malted barley flour.
Add red wine vinegar, olive oil, and warm water to the dry mixture and stir until a sticky dough forms.
Transfer dough to a lightly floured surface and knead for about 6 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise at room temperature for 1 hour or until doubled in size.
Punch down the risen dough and divide it into 6 equal portions.
Roll each portion into a thin circle, about 60 mm in diameter, using a rolling pin and a little flour to prevent sticking.
Heat a nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and lightly oil it with a paper towel.
Cook each flatbread for 1 to 2 minutes on each side until golden brown and slightly puffy, brushing the top with a dab of olive oil if desired.
Serve warm, garnished with a sprinkle of za'atar or a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.
